description: Figure 11 presents a line-chart prediction effect graph comparing Actual YS (gray line) and Predicted YS (red line) across approximately 120 sequential samples indexed on the horizontal axis, with the vertical axis showing yield strength values from 0 to 3000 as reported in the study. The plot validates the Genetic Algorithm-optimized Extremely Randomized Trees (GA-Extra Trees) model for medium-manganese low-density steel, specifically the Fe-0.25C-3Mn-2Al-0.4Si system investigated under intercritical annealing temperatures of 750, 800, and 850 °C, together with complementary literature data. The red predicted curve closely tracks the gray actual curve throughout the sample series, accurately capturing pronounced peaks near samples ~10, ~30, ~45, and ~115 and mirroring intermediate fluctuations with only minor deviations. This strong visual agreement supports the paper's central conclusion that the integrated data-driven multi-mechanism strengthening approach reliably predicts yield strength across diverse processing conditions.
